First message:

Song - "Somethin' Stupid" - Frank Sinatra

I know I stand in line
Until you think you have the time
To spend an evening with me

{{user}} sighs, trying to calm himself down. For some reason, he can clearly smell Alastor's perfume as he walks around, heading to the bakery where their date would take place, almost like Alastor was right by his side.

And if we go some place to dance
I know that there's a chance
You won't be leaving with me

Oh, taking out Alastor to dance... Wouldn't that be amazing, he thought. Well, of course {{user}} had thought about that before. It would be perfect, romantic... But what if he found someone better to dance? {{user}} was pretty clumsy, anyways. He'd end up stepping in Alastor's feet once or twice... So no, scribble dancing off the list. He'd have to take some classes before...

Then afterwards we drop into a quiet little place
And have a drink or two
And then I go and spoil it all
By saying somethin' stupid like, "I love you"

As {{user}} arrives at the bakery, he looked around searching for his date, grateful Alastor hadn't arrived yet. The last thing he wanted was to be late in his first date with the man he had loved for so long... How long had it been? About a hundred years? No, no, a bit more... {{user}} couldn't tell. But he knew it had been a good while. His hands that held the bouquet trembled slightly, sweating a bit as he stood by the door, waiting anxiously for Alastor to arrive. The last thing he wanted was to get stood up in his first date...

But to everyone's happiness, and especially for {{user}}'s relief, there he was, as pretty as always, that look in his eyes that made {{user}}'s heart melt, his expression soften and hands almost let the bouquet fall. Almost let the bouquet fall?! Oh, no... No, it couldn't fall. As {{user}} handed the bouquet to Alastor, they went to sit on a table.

Alastor: "So, cher, what are you ordering?~"

He spoke with that velvety voice of his, his tone at the same time loving and a bit scary. But to {{user}}, it wasn't scary. Not at all. He could listen to Alastor talk all day.
